The board changed but nothing visible changed in the button or shape regions. Let me check what actually changed by comparing the before and after frames more carefully. The board_changed flag was True but the visible regions look the same. Maybe something subtle changed - perhaps the step counter or some other HUD element.
Let me look at the transition to see what actually changed.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Clicking on the yellow shape caused board_changed=True but the visible button/shape regions look identical. I need to find what actually changed. Let me compare before/after frames more carefully.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after frames to find what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

# Compare segmentation
before_nodes = before.segmentation['nodes']
after_nodes = after.segmentation['nodes']

print(f"Before: {len(before_nodes)} nodes, After: {len(after_nodes)} nodes")

# Find differences
before_hashes = [(n['id'], n['hash'], n['color'], n['pixels']) for n in before_nodes]
after_hashes = [(n['id'], n['hash'], n['color'], n['pixels']) for n in after_nodes]

# Check for any differences
for i, (b, a) in enumerate(zip(before_hashes, after_hashes)):
    if b != a:
        print(f"Node {i} changed: {b} -> {a}")

# Also check if node count changed
if len(before_nodes) != len(after_nodes):
    print(f"Node count changed: {len(before_nodes)} -> {len(after_nodes)}")

# Check step
print(f"Before step: {before.step}, After step: {after.step}")

# Check the blue shape area more carefully
lines_before = before.ascii.split('\n')
lines_after = after.ascii.split('\n')

print("\nBlue shape region (rows 48-56):")
for i in range(48, 57):
    b_line = lines_before[i] if i < len(lines_before) else ""
    a_line = lines_after[i] if i < len(lines_after) else ""
    if b_line != a_line:
        print(f"Row {i} CHANGED:")
        print(f"  Before: {b_line}")
        print(f"  After:  {a_line}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
